This document contains the complete field observations, constituency-wise reports, leadership interactions, issue assessments, and organisational findings gathered during my visit to Nandyala District as part of the Sangathan Srijan Abhiyan.All reports, aspirant details, leadership feedback, and constituency-level assessments have been compiled in a uniform AICC reporting format for submission and internal review.This file also includes:•Detailed analysis of the district’s organisational gaps•Cadre feedback and political ground realities•DCC President aspirants’ profiles (three shortlisted individuals)•My observer notes and assessment-based recommendations•Field-level issues raised by workers, leaders, and community groupsThe attached reports reflect on-the-ground realities and are prepared with neutrality, transparency, and adherence to AICC guidelines.Final decisions regarding appointments, selections, or restructuring lie solely with the All India Congress Committee (AICC).Submitted respectfully,N. A. HarisAICC Member

ToSh. K.C. Venugopal JiOrganising General Secretary, AICCNew DelhiRespected Sir,In accordance with the directions of the AICC, I, Jagdish Jangid, former Member of Legislative Assembly, Rajasthan, was assigned as Observer for the Sangathan Srijan Abhiyaan 2025 for District Hisar, Haryana.During my recent extensive visit covering all seven assembly segments — Adampur, Uklana, Narnaud, Hansi, Barwala, and Nalwa — I interacted in detail with grassroots party workers, sitting MLAs, MLA candidates, senior Congress leaders, and local office bearers. I also held meaningful discussions with influential leaders including Kumari Shailja Ji, Chaudhary Virendra Singh Ji, Chaudhary Sampat Singh Ji, and other key stakeholders to assess the ground situation and gather practical suggestions for organisational strengthening.---Key ObservationsDemographic Profile of Hisar District:Jat Community: 25–

Shri Pratap Singh (Age 45, ST-Rajput Kinnaura), the Pradesh Convenor of the Kisan Congress, is a long-standing and dedicated grassroots worker since 2005 with proven local electoral success. However, his major weakness is the limited scope of his experience, concentrated in functional wings rather than core DCC administration. Crucially, only a few party members currently propose his name, reflecting his low organizational influence.  while he is highly reliable and sincere, he lacks the necessary high-profile political command and independent force to effectively lead a district unit facing severe factional conflict. He is considered a good organizational hand, but not a dominant leader.

On 25-11-2025, I arrived in the Poonch district of Jammu
On 26 November 2025, as part of the "Sangathan Srijan Abhiyan" in Jammu

On 27 November 2025, at the Dak Bungalow of Poonch district headquarters, a press conference was held to brief the media about the Sangathan Srajan Abhiyan. On the same day, under this campaign, joint and one-to-one meetings were conducted with the office-bearers and workers of Poonch and Mandi blocks of the Haveli assembly constituency to discuss organisational strengthening and gather their feedback. Later in the evening, interactions were also held with representatives of social organisations, civil society, students, and religious leaders of the Haveli assembly area.

On 28 November 2025, under the "Sangathan Srijan Abhiyan" in Poonch district, a one-to-one meeting was held with the office-bearers and senior party workers from the three blocks of the Surankote Assembly constituency — Lasana Block, Surankote Block, and Buffliaz (Bafliaz) Block — to discuss measures for strengthening the organization.

On 29 November 2025, I met social workers and civil-society representatives of the Surankote block in Poonch district to discuss safeguarding the democratic framework, strengthening the Congress Party, addressing youth concerns, and ensuring the development of the Pir Panjal region. The discussions also covered attempts by certain forces to divide voters along Gujjar–Pahari lines during elections, the need to counter such tactics through civic awareness, and concerns over leaders leaving the party and newcomers being given key responsibilities—issues that were highlighted as damaging to public trust and in need of corrective action.

On 02 December 2025, under the Sangthan Srajan Abhiyan, a meeting was held in the Surankote block of Surankote assembly constituency, Poonch district, with social activists, legal activists, members of the teachers’ association and students. Dialogue was conducted with the aim of strengthening the party organisation on the ground. The students requested the formation of a student wing (NSUI) on the district level of the Congress party and the initiation of student-union elections in colleges. Representatives of social organisations observed that many people in the area are deeply devoted to local pirs, faqirs and dargahs, which has helped the current Member of Parliament win for five consecutive generations — themselves belonging to that family. Meanwhile, the teachers raised their personal concern regarding the start of pension and have been protesting for it. Everyone insisted on the need for support from the Congress party in their social and political struggle, hoping that with people joining the party, it could once again win a place in their hearts.

On 3 December 2025, meetings were held in Motihady village of the Surankote Assembly constituency and later in the evening at Khadi Karmada village of Poonch Block in the Haveli Assembly constituency, where block- and booth-level workers and office-bearers were briefed about the Sangathan Srijan Abhiyan and engaged in discussions to strengthen the Congress Party at the grassroots level.

जिले मे सबसे अच्छा और उपयुक्त नाम जो कांग्रेस संघठन अब की स्थिति मे चला सकते है।
जिले मे ब्राह्मण समाज के होने की वजह से और ग्रामीण परिवेश से जिलाध्यक्ष के लिए दुसरे सबसे उपयुक्त व्यक्ति हो सकते है।
कुर्मी बहुलय छेत्र होने की वजह से तीसरे सबसे उपयुक्त जिलाध्यक्ष के उमीदवार।
दबंग व साफ छवि होने के कारण व धन से सक्षम है इसलिए चौथे सबसे जिलाध्यक्ष के लिए उपयुक्त कैंडिडेट।
वकील और विधानसभा पनागर से उमीदवार होने की वजह से पांचवा सबसे उपयुक्त कैंडिडेट जिलाध्यक्ष के लिए।
युवा और यादव समाज जो की जिले मे एक अच्छी तादाद मे छठे नंबर के सबसे उपयुक्त जिलाध्यक्ष के लिए उम्मीदवार।
युवा है जोश है और वर्तमान मे जिला पंचायत सदस्य है आगे चलकर पार्टी के लिए अच्छा काम कर सकता है।
दलित वर्ग से आते है और इनको प्रदेश या जिले मे कोई जिम्मेदारी मिलनी चाहिए।
किसान होने की वजह से और वरिष्ठ कांग्रेसी की वजह से इनको जिले मे एडजस्ट करना चाहिए।
पाटन से विधानसभा प्रतियाशी रहे इनको प्रदेश मे एडजस्ट किया जाये।
किसान नेता है अनुभवी है इनको किसान कांग्रेस मे प्रदेश मे एडजस्ट करके इनकी सेवा लेनी चाहिए।
पुरानी कांग्रेसी कार्यकर्ता है और बहुत अच्छा बोलती है इनको प्रदेश मे प्रवक्ता का पद मिलना चाहिए।
पुरानी कांग्रेसी कार्यकर्ता है और दलित वर्ग से आते है इनको कांग्रेस sc सेल मे सेवा देनी चाहिए।
युवा नेता है इनको जिले मे पार्टी की सेवा करनी चाहिए।
ब्लॉक अध्यक्ष है और अभी और मेहनत की जरूरत वर्तमान मे ब्लॉक या जिले मे इनका इस्तेमाल होना चाहिए।
दलित नेता है और सरल सवभाव के है इनको प्रदेश sc डिपार्टमेंट मे जिम्मेदारी दी जानी चाहिए।
वरिष्ठ कांग्रेसी है इनको जिले मे जिम्मेदारी मिलनी चाहिए।
पूर्व जिलाध्यक्ष है इनको प्रदेश मे कोई जिम्मेदारी मिलनी चाहिए।
अच्छे नेता है और वरिष्ठ भी हज इनको जिले या प्रदेश मे कोई महत्पूर्ण जिम्मेदारी दी जाये।

Letter of 5 DCC Presidents including Mon , regarding no political activities due to FNTA issue, for which I was not allowed to visit the assemblies despite my several requests
The excel file is having 11 spreadsheets which analyses Party position in the booths as per form 20 in the last three assembly elections in all 9 assemblies of Mon district and its booth wise results
The attached pdf is collected from the local District administrative office and is about the booth and village mapping of all assemblies of Mon district.
It includes list of BLAs from 4 assemblies
The file includes details of various Naga unions, including Student Unions which are very influential and take part in the political decision making process
Village Councils are very important in Mon as it follows traditional local governance model. The file includes details of Village Council and their Chairmen's contact details
ASHA workers are very much connected with the local women and their groups. The list includes ASHA workers contact details of Mon District
Village Development Secretaries are very influential in Mon District. People are completely dependent on them for Govt schemes. The list includes Village Development Secretaries details.

As Mon is overwhelmingly Christian District, where people are deeply associated with Churches, mainly the Baptists. Pastors are playing very important roles. The file includes details of Pastors and other Church officials

Mon is a highly challenging district, where party has minimum presence, Roads are not even motorable, the district sharing borders with Myanmar etc.  As SSA observers, I got opportunity to meet several persons besides political ones, which enriched my experience and knowledge.The report includes lot of data, contact details, candidates and their status, future road map, Form 20 analysis etc

General Issues related to Fazilka District1.Shri Ravinder Kumar (Sarpanch, Village - Kheowali), Mob no. 9501149551Key issues:    1. local school in Kheowali be upgraded to a higher secondary level2. Needed  Railway Underpass.In Kheowali village, the existing school provides education only up to the 5th grade. For higher classes, children are compelled to travel 3 Km to a nearby higher secondary school, which requires crossing a railway track. This situation poses serious safety concerns, as young students are forced to cycle or walk across the track daily, creating constant risk for their lives. Recognising this issue, the village sarpanch has strongly demanded that the local school in Kheowali be upgraded to a higher secondary level, up to the 12th grade, and that a Railway Underpass be constructed to ensure the safe movement of students and villagers.2. Shri Devi Lal (Sarpanch, Village - Sardarpura), Mob no. 8872893205Key Issues :   1. Poor Drinking Water issue2. Poor road conditions3. Shortage of teachers in govt. schoolsIn Sardarpura village, residents have been facing severe drinking water problems for a long time. The condition of roads remains poor, making daily movement difficult. Schools are also suffering due to an acute shortage of teachers, affecting the quality of education and leaving students at a disadvantage.3.Group Discussion – Baluana Block SarpanchsShri Pritam Singh (Sarpanch), Mob no. 9478901760 and Shri Prem Singh (Sarpanch- Village Chugge Gulab Singh), mob no. 7505153900  10 other Sarpanchs Key Issues:   1. Malki (Land ownership) issue 2. Contaminated Water resulted in rising cause of serious health issues 3. Poor  educational infrastructure3. Shortage of teachers in govt. schools4. Drug addiction and smuggling of drugs in border areas5.  MNREGA scheme has not been functional for the last 4 monthsA group discussion was held with the sarpanchs of Baluana Block, where several critical issues concerning the border area were highlighted. The members strongly insisted that the long-pending Malki (land ownership) issue be addressed. They raised grave concerns over contaminated water from Jalandhar and Ludhiana mixing into the rivers of Baluana, which has resulted in alarming health consequences, including rising cases of cancer, Hepatitis-B, early deaths, calcium deficiency, knee and joint problems, and dental issues. The delegation also underlined the absence of proper educational infrastructure, coupled with a serious deficit of teachers, hampering the future of rural children. Drug addiction was flagged as a crucial challenge, with a strong demand for setting up a rehabilitation centre and taking strict action against the smuggling of drugs in border areas.Additionally, they expressed disappointment that the MNREGA scheme has not been functional for the last four months, depriving villagers of much-needed work opportunities.The sarpanchs urged that these issues be taken up on priority to safeguard the health, livelihood, and future of the people of Baluana.4.Discussion with Police Personnels – Punjab Police Department ASI Tejinder Jeet Singh, Mob no. 9814800164, ASI Balkalan Singh, mob. no. 9814166109During the interaction with ASI and police personnel, it was conveyed that the government’s assurance of introducing 8-hour duty shifts for police staff has not been implemented, causing prolonged working hours. They further highlighted that Dearness Allowance (DA) is not being disbursed properly.The police personnel also raised serious concern over the withdrawal of multiple benefits by the present government, including rural allowance, border allowance, uniform allowance, special pay allowance, and nearly 38 other allowances. These cuts have adversely affected the morale and welfare of the Punjab Police force, who are already working under difficult conditions.It was also pointed out that former Finance Minister Manpreet Singh Badal failed to implement the 6th Pay Commission recommendations, which led to widespread dissatisfaction among government employees. This neglect was identified as a major reason why employees turned against the previous government, resulting in the victory of the Aam Aadmi Party in Punjab.The discussion emphasised the urgent need for the government to restore allowances, implement promised reforms, and ensure fair treatment of employees, as their trust and support are crucial for the state’s stability and governance.5.Interaction with the congress leaders Shri Summer Beer Singh Sidhu, AAP Assembly Candidate in 2017, joined congress in 2022, mob no. 8100000137During a detailed discussion with Shri Summer Beer Singh Sidhu, AAP MLA candidate in 2017 Assembly election, now he joined Congress Party in 2022. He expressed that the AAP government initially came to power with a strong wave of hope and high expectations among the public. However, over time, allegations of corruption and the government’s inability to fulfill most of its manifesto promises have led to widespread disillusionment among citizens. He noted that the government has also failed to generate significant revenue for the state treasury, further limiting its ability to implement welfare programs.From an administrative and public perception perspective, he observed that the general populace increasingly sees the current governments led by Channi and Bhagwant Mann as being largely the same, which has resulted in growing frustration and dissatisfaction with the ruling party.He reflected on Captain Amarinder Singh’s tenure, stating that his first term was comparable to the effective governance of Rana Pratap Singh, whereas his second term did not meet expectations.The MLA candidate also highlighted that the AAP government had planned a land pooling scheme aimed at creating residential and commercial plots for landowners. Under the proposal, 1000 to 1500 acres of land would be acquired from landowners, and in return, each landowner would receive a 1,000 sq. yard residential flat and a 200 sq. yard commercial building. This scheme was intended to fund the government’s promise of providing Rs. 1,100 per month to women, but due to strong protests from agriculture activists, the scheme could not be implemented.Overall, he stressed that while AAP came to power with high hopes, administrative challenges, policy delays, and unimplemented schemes have significantly eroded public confidence in the government6.Discussion with Ex-MLA Shri Nathu Ram, Mob. no. 9417263142 During a personal discussion with Ex-MLA Shri Nathu Ram, he emphasised his contributions during his tenure as MLA. He highlighted that he successfully connected 122 villages with filtered water pipelines through a major project costing approximately ₹563 crore, ensuring safe drinking water for rural communities. However, he expressed concern that the current AAP government is not maintaining this project, leading to operational issues and affecting the beneficiaries.He also noted that he was instrumental in establishing an Arts College in his assembly constituency with an investment of around ₹4 crore, improving higher education access for local students. Shri Nathu Ram stressed the importance of maintaining and upgrading past developmental initiatives to continue serving the people effectively.7.General Issues and Suggestions – Fazilka DistrictDuring the discussions with local Congress leaders in Fazilka district, several pressing issues were highlighted:1.Flood Management: Leaders suggested that if a proper channel and dam construction plan is implemented, future flood-related damages could be effectively managed and controlled, providing long-term safety to the people.2.Agricultural Challenges: The rising groundwater level has compelled Kinnow growers to shift towards paddy cultivation, creating an unsustainable situation. Leaders pointed out that currently, no effective solution has been devised to address this challenge.3.Border Area Issues: Between the fencing and the zero line, agricultural land belonging to Indian farmers is accessible only between 9 AM to 5 PM. During this period, the cattle from Pakistan frequently enter and destroy crops, causing heavy losses to farmers.The leaders urged immediate intervention to protect farmlands, support farmers, and plan sustainable flood control measures to safeguard the livelihoods of Fazilka’s residents.

General points :1. After seeing the flood affected areas and discussion with the farmers and leaders I feel construction of dam and construction of canal to divert excessively flowing flood water towards Rajasthan.This may avoid damages to the craps of Fazilka district.The excess water can be utilised for the Rajasthan farmers.2.The Revenge politics is more in Punjab. Whoever in power used to influence the police to file false FIRs against their political opponents to suppress them. It is very commonly observed in Fazilka district.

सुखजीत सिंह काका लोहगढ़ निवर्तमान जिला कांग्रेस कमेटी के अध्यक्ष हैं । ये शिरोमणि अकाली दल से 2011 मे कांग्रेस मे  आयें। ये कांग्रेस मे विभिन्न पदों पर रहें । वर्तमान समय मे इनकी उम्र 72 वर्ष हैं । जिसके कारण इनकी सक्रियता कम हैं । लेकिन कांग्रेस को इनको संगठन के किसी अन्य मोर्चे पर सेवा का मौका देना चाहिए । यह जमीनी स्तर पर मजबूत हैं

लेफ्टिनेंट कर्नल बाबू सिंह वैचारिक रूप से प्रतिबद्ध कांग्रेसी हैं। पुर्व DCC प्रेसीडेंट रह चूकें हैं। वर्तमान समय मे इनकी उम्र 82 वर्ष हैं । लेकिन ये शारीरिक और मानसिक रूप से बिल्कुल स्वस्थ्य हैं । प्रदेश कांग्रेस कमेटी को इनको सैनिक सेल मे रखकर इनकी  सेवा लेनी चाहिए ।

स्वर्ण सिंह की उम्र 70 वर्ष है । ये वैचारिक रूप से प्रतिबबद्ध कांग्रेसी हैं। दलित समुदाय के अधिकारों को लेकर ये सचेत हैं। प्रदेश कांग्रेस कमेटी को दलितों के मुद्दों पर मुखरता से आवाज रखने के लिए इनकी सेवा लेनी चाहिए ।

लाभ सिंह , मोगा के वरिष्ठ समाजसेवी और शिक्षा के क्षेत्र से जुड़े बड़े नाम हैं । इन्हे सरकार के साथ काम करने का एक लंबा अनुभव है। प्रदेश कांग्रेस कमेटी को लाभ सिंह को संगठन में कहीं समायोजित करना चाहिए ।
